title: Why Novartis CEO ended his ADC holdout for $1.5B Myricx deal
summary: For years, as Big Pharma poured billions into ADCs, Novartis CEO Vas Narasimhan notably stayed on the sidelines. Asked by Fierce during Novartis’ Q2 earnings call, Narasimhan explained why he is ending that holdout with the proposed $1.5 billion deal for Myricx.

title: Jupiter enters psychedelics space with $100M deal for PharmAla's MDMA drug
summary: Jupiter Neurosciences will pay $3.3 million upfront to Canada's PharmAla Biotech for U.S. rights to an MDMA drug to treat neuropsychiatric disorders.

title: Agios says adios to sickle cell program over lackluster phase 2 data
summary: A phase 2 study of Agios Pharmaceuticals’ next-generation pyruvate kinase activator has failed to support continued development in sickle cell disease, leading the company to ax an indication for the second time in two months.

title: Indian generics maker Lupin seeds US spinout Kaveri with cash, clinical-phase cancer assets
summary: Lupin has spun out two clinical-phase cancer drug candidates to create Kaveri Therapeutics, a U.S.-based startup that plans to raise cash for further development. Kaveri’s programs hit targets pursued by major drugmakers including AstraZeneca, Bayer and Bristol Myers Squibb.

title: Novartis drops phase 2 radioligand therapy, but CEO’s commitment to modality ‘unchanged’
summary: Novartis has dropped a phase 2-stage radioligand therapy after the candidate disappointed in the clinic.

title: Genetic medicine luminaries launch new center for personalized therapies
summary: A team of veteran scientists from elite institutions has come together to form a new center dedicated to the crafting of personalized genetic medicines, adding to the growing list of similar efforts in the wake of Baby KJ’s groundbreaking custom CRISPR treatment.

title: ‘There is always a first’: Why US biotech Axiom will begin its IPO journey in Hong Kong
summary: Axiom Biosciences intends to pursue an IPO on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange (HKEX), followed by a co-listing on the Nasdaq—a strategy the company claims is the first U.S. biotech to use.

title: Roche taps Foundation Medicine’s chief as new North America Diagnostics lead, creates new commercial role
summary: Dan Malarek is leaving his role as chief executive of Roche’s sequencing subsidiary Foundation Medicine as he takes up the mantle as President and CEO of Roche Diagnostics North America.

title: BMS expands AI footprint with Nvidia, aiming for ‘most powerful AI factory in life sciences’
summary: Bristol Myers Squibb has entered into an expanded collaboration with Nvidia to create the biggest supercomputer for drug R&D, according to the announcement.

title: Tempus buys out cancer genomics partner Personalis in $1.5 billion deal
summary: Tempus is boosting its cancer offerings with a deal to buy out Personalis three years after the pair first teamed up in oncology.

title: Scribe aims for $96M IPO to fund clinical push for lipid-lowering genetic meds
summary: Scribe Therapeutics has put its IPO plans down on paper, showing the genetic medicine company hopes to raise $96 million through its public listing.

title: Kolon’s cell-based gene therapy fails phase 3 osteoarthritis trial
summary: A phase 3 trial of Kolon TissueGene’s cell-based gene therapy has missed its co-primary endpoints. With a second late-stage study scheduled to report data in October, the biotech is holding off on determining the next steps for the osteoarthritis candidate.

title: De-Risking a Rare Disease Launch Starts with Precision
summary: Why traditional models fall short in rare disease commercialization—and what works instead.

title: Why early de-risking is redefining the path to IND
summary: Lonza explains why early de-risking has become a strategic imperative for biotech teams working to reach IND with confidence.

title: Understanding Agentic AI in Clinical Development
summary: Earlier this year, my colleague Matt Harrington suggested that clinical leaders should stop asking whether their organizations have an “AI strategy”, not because strategy doesn’t matter, but because the question often produces the wrong conversation.

title: Amgen agrees to $74M settlement to resolve investor lawsuit over potential $10.7B tax bill
summary: Amgen has agreed to a $74 million settlement with investors to resolve a lawsuit that alleged the company hid a $10.7 billion tax bill from the public and kept the company's share price artificially high from July of 2020 to April of 2022.

title: Sanofi continues tweaks to executive roster as new CEO Garijo settles in
summary: With former Merck KGaA CEO Belén Garijo, M.D., Ph.D., now calling the shots at Sanofi, the French drugmaker continues to refine its executive roster, albeit with fewer high-profile C-suite departures now than earlier in the year.

title: Alembic site slapped with FDA warning letter over patient consent concerns
summary: Following an inspection earlier this year that yielded a Form 483, Indian generics juggernaut Alembic Pharmaceuticals has received a harsher reprimand from the U.S. FDA.

title: Lilly, BMS aerospace partner Redwire opens new site in Indiana
summary: Aerospace technology company Redwire—which has partnered with BMS and Eli Lilly to test drug development and manufacturing in space—has opened a new 30,000-square-foot research and microgravity payload development facility in Indiana.

title: Novartis CEO brushes off oral PCSK9 competition as Leqvio drives Q2 growth
summary: Novartis' CEO remains unfazed by the threat of a newly approved oral PCSK9 inhibitor in Merck & Co.’s Lipfendra, doubling down on the long-term potential of his company's injectable cholesterol drug, Leqvio.

title: Gilead, Merck’s phase 3 data back first weekly HIV pill
summary: Detailed phase 3 results from two Islend clinical trials confirmed that a once-weekly, single-tablet combination of Merck’s islatravir and Gilead’s lenacapavir matches daily treatments like Biktarvy at maintaining HIV viral suppression

title: Novo Nordisk files lawsuit accusing rival Eli Lilly of running ‘false and misleading’ GLP-1 ads
summary: Novo Nordisk has accused Eli Lilly of running ad campaigns containing “deliberately false” messages that mislead the public about the comparative efficacy of GLP-1 medicines.

title: J&J’s Tremfya remains on top in Q2 pharma ad spending league as overall outlay drops
summary: Johnson & Johnson and AbbVie look set for a continued pharma ad spending battle for the second half of 2026 as the U.S. Big Pharma’s immunology drug Tremfya took the top spot for a second quarter in a row, according to data from iSpot.tv.

title: Sanofi survey uncovers Type 1 diabetes misconceptions and screening gaps
summary: Widespread misconceptions about Type 1 diabetes could be contributing to a reluctance to get screened for the autoimmune disease, according to a survey Sanofi commissioned.

title: J&J, Time link up to spotlight healthcare leadership with Healthcare Champion of the Year award
summary: The Healthcare Champion of the Year, to be selected by J&J and a panel of industry experts, will be recognized at Time’s “A Year in TIME” celebration in December.

title: Samsung Biologics spreads wings with proposed $1.8B acquisition of Swiss CDMO PolyPeptide
summary: South Korean contract manufacturer Samsung Biologics has tapped into the booming peptide production and weight loss market with plans to buy out Switzerland-based CDMO PolyPeptide for 1.46 billion Swiss francs ($1.8 billion).

title: Why Legend Biotech stock fell 10% despite record Carvykti sales
summary: On paper, it was another strong quarter for Carvykti. Johnson & Johnson reported record sales for the CAR-T therapy, signaling robust demand in multiple myeloma. Yet record revenue wasn’t enough to spare its partner, Legend Biotech, from a 10% stock drop, thanks in part to co

title: Regulatory tracker: Takeda's Qdenga becomes 1st dengue vaccine cleared in India
summary: In this tracker, Fierce Pharma is recording the regulatory progress of in-market products, including expansions into key geographies and new indications. Some of these updates may not meet the bar for standalone stories, but we think they are still worth mentioning.

title: Why modern clinical trials need a finance-first strategy
summary: As trial complexity rises, finance modernization is becoming a strategic imperative for speed, site trust and operational resilience.

title: Why Commercial Evidence Is the Next Breakthrough in Pharma Intelligence
summary: Clinical teams have long used real world evidence to understand how a drug works. Now commercial teams can mine evidence from unstructured data to drive action.

title: Trump administration says it’s deferring $1B in Medicaid payments to California and Minnesota
summary: The Trump administration said it was deferring more than $1 billion in Medicaid payments to Minnesota and California because of “suspected fraud and noncompliance.”

summary: The administration is shifting some funding to states, but community groups are set to lose out.

title: STAT+: Nabla’s new CEO on plan to gain market share in a competitive AI scribe environment
summary: Ambient scribe developer Nabla said Brian Manning will be the Paris-based company's new CEO as it looks to grow in a highly competitive market.

title: STAT+: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly over GLP-1 drug ads it calls ‘deceptive’
summary: Novo Nordisk sued Eli Lilly for allegedly running a “deceptive” ad campaign that uses “outdated clinical trials” to make its obesity and diabetes drugs appear more effective.

title: Opinion: Science and the public are learning the wrong lessons from Covid
summary: “Post-pandemic polarization threatens to lock our memories into attractive, untrue conclusions that will leave us unprotected next time,” write Marc Lipsitch and Sara Cody.

summary: The proposed rule could “weaken the NIH’s benign influence over research risks,” writes Doni Bloomfield.

title: THC drinks are sold beside sodas, iced tea, and even chocolate milk, often in violation of state laws
summary: THC drinks, which have exploded in popularity in recent years, are sold in retail stores beside sodas, iced tea, and even chocolate milk, often in violation of state laws.
